title: PES - Pandemic Emergency Scenario
description: The Pandemic Emergency Scenario (PES) dataset comprises neurophysiological and behavioral data from 9 participants engaged in a complex resource allocation task during a simulated pandemic emergency. Participants, paired with an artificial agent, made sequential decisions about allocating limited medical resources across affected cities while reporting confidence in their choices. EEG recordings, electrocardiography, and electrooculography were collected simultaneously with behavioral responses across 64 experimental sequences.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a Biosemi ActiveTwo system with 64 electrodes positioned according to the International 10-20 System, sampled at 2048 Hz. Eight additional external channels recorded electrocardiography (2 channels) and electrooculography (4 channels). Participants performed resource allocation decisions via mouse input over a 10-second response window, followed by a 5-second confidence rating phase using mouse wheel input.

funding_references.funder_name: UK Ministry of Defence (MOD) via funding from US/UK DoD BARI

funding_references.award_title: US/UK DoD Bilateral Academic Research Initiative (BARI)
